message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="red" summary="Step 9 a11y design feedback, 2 blocking items">
Heydon here with accessibility feedback on the Step 9 design.

CONSENSUS: APPROVED on Luca continuing as Driver.

**Design feedback:**

The existing clarify form (`templates/inbox_item.html`) uses a solid accessible pattern:
- `<fieldset>` with visually-hidden `<legend>` ("Clarify: [item title]")
- Radio buttons for destination selection ("Next Action", "Project")
- CSS `:has()` progressive disclosure for project-specific fields
- Visible labels on revealed fields

Adding "Waiting For" as a third radio option should follow the same pattern. Specific guidance:

**1. New radio button** — Add to the existing radio group (line 22 area):
```html
<label class="inbox-item__radio-label">
    <input type="radio" name="clarify_type" value="waiting_for" class="inbox-item__radio">
    Waiting For
</label>
```

**2. Waiting For fields** — Add a new `div` (similar to `.inbox-item__project-fields`) that is hidden by default and revealed via CSS `:has()`:
```css
.inbox-item__waiting-for-fields {
    display: none;
    /* ... */
}
.inbox-item__clarify-fieldset:has(input[value="waiting_for"]:checked) .inbox-item__waiting-for-fields {
    display: flex;
}
```

**3. Visible labels required** — The revealed fields need visible `<label>` elements for "Who or what?" (the waiting_on field). The title is pre-filled from the inbox item, so that field may not be needed — clarify with the team whether the inbox item title becomes the waiting-for title directly.

**4. Context field visibility** — Currently the context `<select>` is in `.inbox-item__shared-fields` which is always visible. But context is NOT relevant to Waiting For items (they don't have contexts). When "Waiting For" is selected, the context field should be hidden. This needs CSS:
```css
.inbox-item__clarify-fieldset:has(input[value="waiting_for"]:checked) .inbox-item__shared-fields {
    display: none;
}
```
This is BLOCKING if not handled — showing an irrelevant context field when "Waiting For" is selected is confusing for all users, and particularly problematic for screen reader users who would encounter a required field that has no bearing on their action.

**5. No-JS fallback** — The CSS `:has()` approach works without JS, which is correct. The form submission handler on the server needs to handle `clarify_type=waiting_for` and ignore the context field for that path.

**Summary of pre-implementation blocking items:**
- BLOCKING: Hide the context field when "Waiting For" radio is selected
- BLOCKING: Visible label on the waiting_on input field

Everything else follows the established pattern and should work cleanly.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Heydon's review is also from the design phase. Both blocking items were addressed in the implementation. Let me confirm.

input.content:
Thanks Heydon. Acknowledging both blocking items and all 5 points:

**BLOCKING 1: Hide context field when "Waiting For" is selected** -- Implemented. CSS rule hides `.inbox-item__shared-fields` when `input[value="waiting_for"]:checked` via `:has()`. Backend also makes `context_id` optional (`Option<Uuid>`) so the waiting_for branch never reads it.

**BLOCKING 2: Visible label on waiting_on input** -- Implemented. The template has:
```html
<label for="waiting-on-{{ item.id }}" class="inbox-item__waiting-on-label">
    Who or what are you waiting on?
</label>
<input type="text" id="waiting-on-{{ item.id }}" name="waiting_on" ... />
```
Label is properly associated via `for`/`id` pairing.

Points 1-3: Radio button added in the existing group, waiting-for fields div follows the project-fields pattern with CSS `:has()` progressive disclosure, and yes -- the inbox item title transfers directly to the WaitingForItem (no separate title input needed).

Point 5: No-JS fallback works correctly. Server ignores context_id for the waiting_for path. Empty waiting_on returns 204 (HTMX) or redirect (non-HTMX).

Implementation is complete and ready for your code review. The template is at `templates/inbox_item.html` and CSS at `static/css/main.css` (search for `waiting-for-fields`).
input.summary: Both blocking a11y items addressed, confirming all points
